skill.name: prototype-to-production
skill.description: Convert HTML, CSS, and Figma-style prototypes into typed, production-ready frontend components. The skill guides prototype analysis, design token extraction, component decomposition, accessibility checks, and integration planning for React and TypeScript projects.
skill.summary: Turn design prototypes into typed, accessible frontend components.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, weak cryptography, credential, and combined-risk patterns, but review found these are documentation and template false positives. The skill contains Markdown examples, SVG namespace URLs, and design token wording, with no evidence of real shell execution, external requests, secret access, weak crypto use, or prompt injection. Publication is reasonable with a low-risk filesystem warning because the workflow reads project files and generates component files.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Project Inspection and Code Generation
low_findings.description: The workflow asks the agent to inspect package.json, TypeScript configuration, styling files, and theme CSS, then provide file placement and component integration guidance. This is legitimate for a prototype conversion skill, but users should review generated file changes before applying them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited workflow explicitly instructs project file inspection and integration guidance. This is normal skill behavior, not malicious activity, so the risk is operational rather than suspicious.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections correspond to Markdown inline code, JSX examples, TypeScript snippets, and conversion tables. No evidence found of executable shell commands, Ruby backticks, child process APIs, or dynamic code execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are rendered documentation or JSX examples. A focused search found no exec, spawn, child_process, eval, or shell invocation APIs in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Network Findings Are Non-Request URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL detections are an external JSON schema identifier and SVG namespace attributes. No evidence found of fetch, axios, curl, webhook endpoints, or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static standards references used for schema and SVG markup. They are not used in code that performs network requests or transmits local data.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Credential Findings Are Token Collisions
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and certificate-key detections appear to match words such as design tokens, theme tokens, and event.key. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, certificate files, private keys, encryption code, or secret handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text uses token terminology for design systems and key terminology for keyboard events. A targeted search found no cryptographic algorithms or credential material.

content.value_statement: Design prototypes often contain duplicated markup, weak structure, and missing accessibility details. This skill turns those prototypes into typed, reusable frontend components that fit the target project.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes HTML, CSS, Figma-style exports, and super-design prototype files.
Detects the target frontend stack from package and TypeScript configuration clues.
Breaks prototype regions into atoms, molecules, and larger reusable components.
Extracts design tokens for color, typography, spacing, radius, shadow, and animation.
Generates typed React component patterns with props, variants, accessibility, and integration guidance.
Maps common HTML attributes, forms, lists, images, SVGs, links, and routing patterns to React equivalents.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a complete automated migration engine.
Generated components still need review against the project design system and build pipeline.
Complex interactions, state machines, and backend integration require project-specific implementation.
Figma support is based on exported markup and class patterns, not direct Figma API access.

use_cases.title: Prototype Cleanup for Frontend Engineers
use_cases.description: Convert a rough HTML or Tailwind prototype into typed components that match an existing React project.

use_cases.title: Design Handoff for Product Teams
use_cases.description: Translate exported design markup into a clear component plan with tokens, variants, and accessibility requirements.

use_cases.title: UI System Expansion for Tech Leads
use_cases.description: Identify repeated patterns in prototype work and turn them into reusable building blocks for a component library.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Simple Prototype
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion of one HTML file
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my prototype HTML file and convert it into production-ready React and TypeScript components. Identify the main components, props, and accessibility updates.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Design Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the prototype styles and extract reusable design tokens for colors, typography, spacing, border radius, shadows, and animation. Show where each token should be used.

prompt_templates.title: Match an Existing Codebase
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced integration with an existing app
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the target project structure and convert this prototype using the existing framework, TypeScript settings, styling approach, and component conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Component Library Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this prototype into reusable atoms, molecules, and organisms. Recommend file placement, component APIs, variant rules, accessibility requirements, and integration steps.

output_examples.input: A single-page HTML prototype with repeated cards, buttons, and form controls.
output_examples.output: A component breakdown with Button, Card, FormField, and PageSection recommendations, including props and accessibility notes.

output_examples.input: A super-design folder containing theme CSS and multiple design iteration files.
output_examples.output: A conversion plan that maps theme variables to design tokens and selects the most reusable prototype patterns.

output_examples.input: A Figma-style export with absolute positioning and generated class names.
output_examples.output: A cleanup strategy that replaces layout artifacts with semantic sections, responsive structure, and reusable component names.

best_practices: Review the target project conventions before generating components.
Extract shared tokens before deciding final component variants.
Verify accessibility, keyboard behavior, and responsive layout after conversion.
anti_patterns: Copy prototype markup directly into production without decomposition.
Preserve generated class names when semantic component names are clearer.
Treat visual similarity as enough without checking behavior and accessibility.

faq.question: Does this skill support React and TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. React with TypeScript is the default output style described by the skill.

faq.question: Can it work with Vue, Svelte, or Angular?
faq.answer: It can detect those frameworks and adapt guidance, but the included templates focus on React.

faq.question: Does it call the Figma API?
faq.answer: No. It works from exported prototype files and recognizable Figma-style markup patterns.

faq.question: Will it edit my project automatically?
faq.answer: The skill provides conversion guidance and templates. Review any generated file changes before applying them.

faq.question: What inputs are most useful?
faq.answer: Provide the prototype HTML, related CSS, theme files, and the target project package configuration.

faq.question: Does it handle accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes semantic HTML, keyboard support, ARIA guidance, and focus management checks.
